decisions.rationale: The gap consists only of a comma and a space, which are punctuation and do not carry any assertive meaning.
The gap contains the conjunction "and", which connects two clauses that are already captured as claims. It does not have independent assertive content.

The gap contains ", whereas ", which establishes a strong contrastive relationship between the preceding and following clauses. This relationship itself may carry assertive meaning that is not fully captured by the adjacent claims alone.
The gap contains the conjunction "and", which connects two phrases that are part of claims. It does not have independent assertive content.
The gap consists only of a period, which is sentence-terminating punctuation and carries no assertive meaning.
The gap contains the conjunction "and", which connects two clauses that are already captured as claims. It does not have independent assertive content.
The gap consists only of a period, which is sentence-terminating punctuation and carries no assertive meaning.
The gap consists of a comma and the conjunction "and", which connect two clauses that are already captured as claims. It does not have independent assertive content.
The gap consists only of a period, which is sentence-terminating punctuation and carries no assertive meaning.
The gap contains the conjunction "and", which connects two clauses that are already captured as claims. It does not have independent assertive content.
The gap consists only of a period, which is sentence-terminating punctuation and carries no assertive meaning.
The gap contains the conjunction "and", which connects two clauses that are already captured as claims. It does not have independent assertive content.

The gap contains ", despite ", which establishes a concessive relationship, indicating that the first clause occurred in spite of the condition in the second clause. This relationship carries assertive meaning that may not be captured by the adjacent claims alone.
The gap consists only of a period, which is sentence-terminating punctuation and carries no assertive meaning.
